title: AuthProvider::auth_header doc missing contract on String validity
description: auth_header() returns Option<(String, String)> but nowhere documents that the name must be a valid HTTP header name (no spaces, lowercase, ASCII) and the value must be valid visible-ASCII. ws/mod.rs calls .parse::<HeaderName>().expect(...) which panics on invalid names. A custom AuthProvider returning a name like 'X-My Header' (with space) would cause a panic at WebSocket connection time with a misleading expect message. The fix is to add explicit constraints to the auth_header() doc comment: 'The header name must be a valid HTTP header name (lowercase ASCII, no spaces). The header value must contain only visible ASCII characters (0x21-0x7E plus HTAB).'
close_reason: Doc constraint added to auth_header() trait method
